ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "lat" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "long" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "closest_city" TEXT /* Name of the closest city to the weather station */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Country code where the weather station is located */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the weather station was installed */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the weather station */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of the weather station (e.g., land, sea, airport) */,
  "sensor_types" TEXT /* Types of sensors used at the weather station */,
  "maintenance_log" TEXT /* Maintenance log for the weather station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_stations_country_code FOREIGN KEY ("country_code") REFERENCES countries ("country_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station where the data was collected */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the data was collected */,
  "temp" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Fahrenheit */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Humidity percentage */,
  "cloudiness" INTEGER /* Cloudiness percentage */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., sensor, API) */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., good, bad, unknown) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_data_source FOREIGN KEY ("data_source") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Country code */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the country belongs to */,
  "climate_type" TEXT /* Climate type of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., sensor, API) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., primary, secondary) */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Temperature, Humidity) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the data file */,
  "checksum" TEXT /* Checksum of the data file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_files_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the weather data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_reason" TEXT /* Reason for accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather report */,
  "report_title" TEXT /* Title of the weather report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station the report is based on */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the data included in the report */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the data included in the report */,
  "report_file_path" TEXT /* File path to the generated report */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report */,
  "recommendations" TEXT /* Recommendations based on the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_reports_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the changes made in this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor type */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the sensor type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "station_sensors" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station */,
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor type */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id", "sensor_type_id")
)
